title: Wet felted slippers
subtitle: Hard-wearing felted slippers sized from a foot tracing using a bubble wrap resist
excerpt: Wet felt a pair of slippers in Corriedale or Romney roving using foot-shaped bubble wrap resists sized from a tracing of your own foot. The finished slippers are firm enough to walk on indoors, water-repellent when new, and re-felt to reshape if they loosen in wear.
sourceNotes: Resist-felted slippers are a standard intermediate project in guild and workshop instruction across Scandinavia and the UK. The foot-tracing sizing method is widely used.

content.text: Felt shrinks roughly 30% during fulling. The

must be cut about 30% larger than the finished foot measurement. For a size 7 (UK) foot of 26 cm length, the resist should be about 34 cm long. Always sample a test strip in the same fibre to confirm your personal shrinkage rate before cutting the resists.

items.name: Corriedale or Romney roving, one colour per pair
Bubble wrap, cut to two foot shapes (30% larger than foot)

content.text: Step 1: Trace and cut the resists

Stand on a sheet of paper and trace around each foot. Measure the length and width of each tracing. Add 30% to each dimension (or your measured shrinkage rate) and draw a new, larger foot shape. Cut two resists from bubble wrap to these enlarged shapes.
Step 2: Layer roving around the resist

Place the resist on the bamboo mat. Cover the top surface with overlapping roving strips in two perpendicular layers, extending 4-5 cm beyond all edges. Wet out with hot soapy water through the felting net. Fold the overhang around to the underside of the resist, add two more roving layers on the underside, and wet out the underside. The overhang from the underside now wraps up onto the top again, completing the enclosure.

Rub in circular strokes through the felting net for 15 minutes per side, working both sides equally. The felt should begin to firm and shrink perceptibly. Once the surface holds together without the net, rub directly with soapy palms, working especially at the edges and toe.
Step 4: Cut the heel opening and remove resist

When the felt passes the pinch test at the heel, cut a horizontal slit about 10 cm long across the heel opening. Reach in and pull out the bubble wrap resist. Put your hand inside the slipper to check the shape and stretch the opening to fit your foot. Return to rubbing with the slipper inside out for a firmer finish, then right-side out to smooth the exterior.

Continue fulling both slippers until they have shrunk to fit your foot measurement (check periodically). Rinse in cool water, press out moisture, and put them on your feet while still damp and warm to shape them to your exact foot. Walk around in them for 5 minutes. Allow to dry on your feet or on a foot-shaped block.

items.fix: Work both slippers simultaneously: alternate between them after each 5-minute session and measure both after each round.
items.cause: Unequal rubbing time or fulling on each piece
items.problem: One slipper is noticeably smaller than the other

items.fix: Check the size by placing the slipper against your foot tracing regularly during the fulling phase. Stop as soon as it reaches your foot measurement.
items.cause: Over-felted before the resist was removed, shrinking past the target size
items.problem: The slipper is too small when checked on the foot

items.fix: After removing the resist, concentrate rubbing at the heel seam area. Work directly on the seam with soapy palms, folding and rubbing the felt together to strengthen it.
items.cause: Insufficient felting at the fold at the heel
items.problem: The heel seam is weak and begins to separate
